data.title: sprint Phase 8 does not invoke /dso:end-session at epic completion
data.description:
## Description

**What**: /dso:sprint Phase 8 (Session Close) performs some cleanup steps internally but does not invoke /dso:end-session. After an epic completes, the user must manually run /dso:end-session to get the full end-of-session workflow.

**Rationalization for current behavior**: Sprint was designed as an inner workflow that executes a single epic. The assumption is that a session may contain multiple sprint invocations (run one epic, then immediately sprint the next), so triggering a full session close after every epic would be premature. Phase 8 performs epic-level cleanup (session summary, technical learnings) but intentionally stops short of session-level teardown — worktree cleanup verification, friction suggestion synthesis, and the full /dso:end workflow — leaving those to an explicit user invocation. This rationale is valid for multi-epic sessions but breaks down for single-epic sessions, which are the common case. The cost is that users who do not know to run /dso:end manually skip worktree cleanup, friction capture, and the structured close.

**Impact**:
- Worktree cleanup check (is_merged + is_clean) never runs automatically
- Friction suggestions from the session are not synthesized
- /dso:end technical learnings step is skipped
- The sprint session summary in Phase 8 is a subset of what /dso:end produces

**Expected behavior**: Phase 8 should either (a) invoke /dso:end-session directly as its final step, or (b) at minimum emit a visible reminder: 'Sprint complete — run /dso:end to close the session.' Option (a) is preferred for single-epic sessions; option (b) preserves multi-sprint chaining.

**Discovered**: 2026-04-13, after completing epic 0e62-ff06 via /dso:sprint.
